name: MadridMetro_S9000
_honesty: The art is a STYLISED RECREATION, drawn from a generated side elevation, not a
tracing of the real vehicle. The numbers below are the real ones, and each says
where it came from. The README and the forum post must say this out loud.
The figures arrived as a table captioned 'valor recomendado', with no source.
Three of them were wrong: capacity was given as 1260 (it is 1274), the cars were
given as a uniform 17.83 m (that is 107/6 - an average wearing a measurement's
coat; the real bodies are 18.425 and 16.880 m), and the weight was not published
by anyone, so it is a guess and is marked as one.

motors.source: https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Series_7000_y_9000 - the 16-motor variant; there is also an 8-motor one at half the power

capacity_total.note: the table we were handed said 1260. It is 1274, and nobody would ever have noticed.

length_cab_car_m.source: https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Series_7000_y_9000 - 'longitud de las cajas de 18.425 para los coches motores con cabina'

length_intermediate_m.note: NOT the 17.83 m we were handed. That number is 107/6 - an average, not a measurement.

height_m.source: https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Series_7000_y_9000 - 3,65 m. The article body also gives 3.870 mm as a MAXIMUM, which will be over roof equipment.

length_carunits.formula: the real cars differ by only 9% (18.425 vs 16.880 m), far below the 1-carunit quantum, so they round to the same length - and equal lengths make every joint (L_prev - L_this)/2 exactly zero
length_carunits.note: unlike the Civia, this is not a compromise. Here the prototype really does have near-equal cars.

weight_total_t.source: a guess. AnsaldoBreda does not publish the mass of the 9000 and no source we found states it. Balance it before anyone plays with it.

reference.note: generated side elevations. NOT orthographic (the camera sits slightly above - the top face of the roof is visible) and NOT flat-shaded, so use them for PROPORTIONS and take absolute sizes from the facts above.
